deep_dive.title: RT-SDGOD: Real-Time Single-Domain Generalized Object Detection
deep_dive.abstract: In real-world deployment under strict real-time constraints, weather and imaging variations induce significant distribution shifts, severely degrading detectors. Single-Domain Generalized Object Detection aims to mitigate this issue, yet existing methods rarely investigate-at the level of problem formulation-the generalization capability of real-time detectors under such constrained inference budgets. To this end, we introduce Real-Time Single-Domain Generalized Object Detection (RT-SDGOD), which focuses on how real-time detectors can achieve cross-domain generalization under zero extra inference overhead by relying solely on training-time representation learning. We observe that, under domain shift, DETR-based real-time detectors mainly degrade through increased missed detections, rooted in limited and unstable object-level discriminative evidence. Based on this, we propose RT-SDGDet, a multi-evidence collaborative modeling framework for RT-SDGOD. The core idea is to enable multiple queries of the same object to collaboratively cover more sufficient discriminative evidence while maintaining the stability of such evidence modeling across views. Specifically, we use one-to-many (O2M) supervision to construct stable object-specific query groups, and further design Discriminative Evidence Diversity Learning (DEDL) and Dual-view Evidence Consistency Learning (DvECL) to expand object-level evidence coverage and improve evidence stability under appearance perturbations, respectively. Since all components are introduced only during training, our method incurs no extra inference overhead. Extensive experiments show that the proposed method achieves better generalization performance than existing approaches across multiple unseen target domains.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Real-time single-domain generalized object detection for streaming video perception.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于流视频感知的实时单域泛化目标检测。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于流视频感知的实时单域泛化目标检测。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于流视频感知的实时单域泛化目标检测。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces real-time single-domain generalized object detection, addressing cross-domain generalization under zero extra inference overhead for streaming perception. Uses training-time representation learning to combat distribution shifts, showing that DETR-based real-time detectors degrade mainly through feature degradation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对实时目标检测器在域偏移下性能严重下降的问题，提出RT-SDGOD任务，专注于在零额外推理开销下实现跨域泛化。通过仅依赖训练时表征学习，观察到DETR基实时检测器主要因特征退化而性能下降，所提方法显著提升实时感知鲁棒性，为流视频理解中的目标检测提供新方案。

quick_skim.title: MotionEnhancer: Leveraging Video Diffusion for Motion-Enhanced Vision-Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: The new era has witnessed a remarkable capability to extend Vision-Language Models (VLMs) for tackling tasks of video understanding. While current VLMs excel at event- or story-level understanding, their ability to capture fine-grained motion details remains limited, primarily due to their focus on high-level static semantic structures and macro-event logic. In contrast, Video Diffusion Models (VDMs) are adept at modeling dynamic motion patterns, benefiting from large-scale video data and the intrinsic requirement of temporal generation. In this paper, we introduce MotionEnhancer, a novel approach that leverages motion priors distilled from a powerful video diffusion model as auxiliary supervision to enhance the motion understanding capability of a VLM via attention alignment. MotionEnhancer comprises two simple parameter-free modules, Motion-sensitive Head Selection (MHS) and Motion-salient Text Token Identification (MTTI), to directly extract and optimize motion-related attentions from the VDM in a computation-only manner. MotionEnhancer provides a scalable solution for motion understanding without additional training parameters, modifications to existing architectures, or tool calling. Extensive experiments demonstrate that MotionEnhancer can achieve consistent improvements over state-of-the-art VLMs on two motion-level video understanding benchmarks, especially on motion-related metrics.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: leverages video diffusion priors to enhance motion understanding in VLMs, benefiting video temporal grounding
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用视频扩散先验增强VLMs的运动理解，有助于视频时序定位
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用视频扩散先验增强VLMs的运动理解，有助于视频时序定位
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用视频扩散先验增强VLMs的运动理解，有助于视频时序定位
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MotionEnhancer distills motion priors from video diffusion models to improve fine-grained motion understanding in Vision-Language Models, bridging a gap in video analysis.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有视觉语言模型（VLM）在视频理解中侧重于高层静态语义和宏事件逻辑，而对细粒度运动细节建模不足的问题，本文提出MotionEnhancer方法，通过从强大的视频扩散模型中蒸馏运动先验知识，作为辅助监督信号来增强VLM的运动理解能力。实验表明，该方法在多个视频理解任务中有效提升了模型对动态运动模式的捕捉，为视频时序定位等下游任务提供了更精细的运动表征。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对现有视觉语言模型（VLM）在视频理解中侧重于高层静态语义和宏事件逻辑，而对细粒度运动细节建模不足的问题，本文提出MotionEnhancer方法，通过从强大的视频扩散模型中蒸馏运动先验知识，作为辅助监督信号来增强VLM的运动理解能力。实验表明，该方法在多个视频理解任务中有效提升了模型对动态运动模式的捕捉，为视频时序定位等下游任务提供了更精细的运动表征。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 视频时序定位，视频理解，流视频理解, especially work that connects or combines: temporal localization in videos; video moment grounding; analyzing videos as they stream online; retrieving moments from videos based on text; real-time perception in streaming video; grounding referring expressions in video with multiple modalities; methods for temporal action localization in untrimmed videos; real time streaming video understanding techniques; video moment retrieval based on natural language queries; online video analysis and event detection approaches.

quick_skim.title: IMAGINE: Adaptive Schema-Imagery Enhanced Composition for Composed Video Retrieval
quick_skim.abstract: Composed Video Retrieval (CVR) is designed to retrieve a target video that matches a reference video modified by a modification text. While existing methods explore cross-modal correspondences, they often assume modified objects appear directly in videos. However, modification texts frequently describe concepts not explicitly presented but implicitly expressed through semantically related visual cues (e.g., "cake" implying "birthday party"). Current approaches typically rely on aligning explicit feature representations within the concrete space, neglecting critical latent associations. To address this, we propose an adaptIve scheMa-ImAGery enhanced composItional NEtwork (IMAGINE). Unlike standard explicit matching, IMAGINE materializes implicit semantics (termed schema imagery) via dynamic multimodal prototypes. These prototypes capture shared latent concepts to adaptively modulate visual features, effectively injecting implicit guidance into the retrieval process. By bridging the gap between explicit visual contents and implicit retrieval intentions, IMAGINE achieves state-of-the-art performance in both CVR and Composed Image Retrieval (CIR) across three widely used benchmarks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: composed video retrieval with text modifications
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 带文本修改的组合视频检索
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 带文本修改的组合视频检索
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 带文本修改的组合视频检索
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces IMAGINE for composed video retrieval by materializing implicit text concepts through schema-imagery enhancement, improving retrieval with indirect semantic cues.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对组合视频检索中文本描述常隐含非直接视觉概念的问题，提出IMAGINE网络，通过自适应模式-意象增强将隐含概念显式化，提升了对间接语义线索的视频检索能力。实验表明，该方法在处理复杂组合查询时表现更优，为基于文本的视频检索提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: TraRA: Trajectory-level Recognition Aggregation for Video Text Spotting in Urban Surveillance
quick_skim.abstract: Video Text Spotting (VTS) is essential for urban surveillance and intelligent transportation systems, enabling automated reading of street signs, vehicle markings, and scene text in video streams. However, reliable recognition remains challenging due to dynamic video factors common in surveillance scenarios, including motion blur, occlusion, and scale variation, which degrade frame-level recognition. Existing VTS methods typically perform recognition independently on each frame, leading to inconsistent and inaccurate results across sequences. To address these limitations, we propose TraRA (Trajectory-level Recognition Aggregation for VTS), a plug-and-play method that performs trajectory-level text recognition by leveraging temporal and multimodal consistency. TraRA integrates two key modules: (1) the Temporal Clustering and (2) the Vision-Language Aggregation. The former refines noisy trajectories by grouping temporally and visually coherent text instances, while the latter employs a Low-Rank Adaptation-enhanced Vision-Language model to fuse visual cues with linguistic context across frames. By aggregating information over entire text trajectories, TraRA achieves robust text recognition even under challenging surveillance conditions. Extensive experiments on four public benchmarks, including road and urban scene datasets (RoadText, BOVText, ArTVideo, and ICDAR15), demonstrate that TraRA consistently improves tracking and recognition performance over state-of-the-art VTS methods. The source code is available at https://github.com/trid2912/TraRA.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: plug-and-play trajectory-level text recognition for surveillance video streams
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 即插即用的轨迹级文本识别方法，用于监控视频流
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 即插即用的轨迹级文本识别方法，用于监控视频流
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 即插即用的轨迹级文本识别方法，用于监控视频流
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: TraRA addresses unreliable frame-level text recognition in surveillance video streams by aggregating recognition results along text trajectories, improving accuracy and consistency across sequences in a plug-and-play manner.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 监控视频中的文本识别常因运动模糊、遮挡等因素导致帧级识别不一致。本文提出TraRA，一种即插即用的轨迹级识别聚合方法，通过沿文本轨迹融合多帧信息提升识别一致性和准确性。在动态视频流中显著改善了文本识别的鲁棒性，为城市监控等在线视频分析任务提供了有效方法。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 监控视频中的文本识别常因运动模糊、遮挡等因素导致帧级识别不一致。本文提出TraRA，一种即插即用的轨迹级识别聚合方法，通过沿文本轨迹融合多帧信息提升识别一致性和准确性。在动态视频流中显著改善了文本识别的鲁棒性，为城市监控等在线视频分析任务提供了有效方法。

quick_skim.title: Counterfactual Reasoning for Fine-Grained Evidence Disentanglement in VideoQA
quick_skim.abstract: Recent advances in video multimodal models have significantly improved VideoQA performance. However, these systems often rely on spurious statistical correlations rather than answer-relevant causal evidence, resulting in unfaithful and brittle reasoning, especially in complex real-world scenarios. Existing methods either rely on cross-modality correlations, costly curated training resources, or insufficient causal assumptions and constraints, and typically operate at the time-interval level. As a result, they fail to explicitly disentangle causal visual cues from confounders and provide limited fine-grained evidence localization. To address this issue, we propose a Counterfactual Reasoning framework for fine-grained Evidence Disentanglement (CREDiT). CREDiT formulates the VideoQA process using a structural causal model and learns cross-modality representations that are explicitly decomposed into causal and non-causal components under independence and minimality constraints. To facilitate faithful disentanglement, we introduce feature-level causal interventions and construct counterfactual inputs that approximate causal effects while suppressing non-causal correlations. Extensive experiments on NExT-GQA, SportsQA, and SPORTU-video demonstrate that CREDiT consistently improves answer accuracy and reasoning reliability across both generic and complex sports scenarios, leading to more trustworthy VideoQA systems.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Addresses fine-grained evidence localization in VideoQA via counterfactual reasoning, directly applicable to temporal localization.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过反事实推理实现视频问答中细粒度证据定位，适用于时序定位任务。
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过反事实推理实现视频问答中细粒度证据定位，适用于时序定位任务。
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过反事实推理实现视频问答中细粒度证据定位，适用于时序定位任务。
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes CRE2D, a counterfactual reasoning framework to disentangle causal visual evidence from confounders for fine-grained evidence localization in VideoQA, improving robustness and interpretability.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 视频多模态模型常依赖伪相关而非真正因果证据，导致推理不稳定且定位不够精细。本文提出反事实推理框架CRE2D，通过结构化因果图分离证据与混淆，实现更精细的视觉证据定位。实验表明该方法在多个VideoQA数据集上提升了证据定位准确度和答案鲁棒性，为视频理解的可解释性提供了新途径。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 视频多模态模型常依赖伪相关而非真正因果证据，导致推理不稳定且定位不够精细。本文提出反事实推理框架CRE2D，通过结构化因果图分离证据与混淆，实现更精细的视觉证据定位。实验表明该方法在多个VideoQA数据集上提升了证据定位准确度和答案鲁棒性，为视频理解的可解释性提供了新途径。
